summary: Iteration 5 testing complete. Backend: 7/7 pytest tests PASS including live SMTP delivery — POST /api/contact with the iteration-5 SMTP verify body (name='SMTP Verify Test', email='hello@trackthebreach.com', reason='Sales', message='Live SMTP delivery test from iteration 5') returned 200 with email_sent:true and was persisted to MongoDB contact_submissions collection. Invalid email returns 422. Frontend desktop: Pricing hero contains 'Pricing built to defend you' + 'today' + 'tomorrow'; subtitle contains 'Pay once' + 'never expire'; all 5 pricing-plan-0..4 cards show 'one-time' (no 'per month'/'monthly') and the emerald 'Credits never expire' badge; desktop navbar items (Product/Solutions/Resources/Company/Pricing/Contact/Log in) have class 'text-[15px] font-semibold'; footer bottom row contains 'Designed & developed by' + clickable 'People's Choice Tech' (data-testid='footer-pct-link', href='https://peopleschoice.tech/', target='_blank'); copyright '© 2026 Track The Breach' present. Frontend mobile (390x844): hamburger at x=12 (left), brand centered (centerX=195 = viewport center), motto banner hidden, brand-motto hidden; drawer is rounded-3xl glass card, 4 accordion sections (product/solutions/resources/company) all aria-expanded=false on initial open (collapsed by default), tapping product opens it, then tapping solutions collapses product (single-open accordion verified), body scroll locks (document.body.style.overflow='hidden') when open and restores ('') when closed via JS-dispatched backdrop click, all bottom buttons present (mobile-pricing, mobile-contact, mobile-login, mobile-signup). ONE BUG FOUND: backdrop overlay click does not close drawer for real users because the backdrop renders with height=0 — the parent <header> uses backdrop-blur-md which creates a containing block for fixed descendants, collapsing the backdrop's `fixed inset-0 top-16` to 0 effective height. The onClick handler is wired correctly (programmatic .click() closes the drawer), but the dim/blur layer is never visible and never receives real pointer events.

ui_bugs.issue: Backdrop overlay <div className='lg:hidden fixed inset-0 top-16 bg-slate-900/30 backdrop-blur-sm z-40 animate-fade-in'> renders with computed height=0 because its parent <header> applies `backdrop-blur-md`, which (per spec) creates a containing block for fixed-positioned descendants. Result: the dim/blur backdrop is invisible AND not clickable. Real users cannot close the drawer by tapping the dim area. Programmatic .click() works because React's onClick is wired, but no real pointer events ever land on a 0-height element. Drawer can still be closed by tapping the X hamburger.

action_items: FIX backdrop containing-block bug: render the mobile drawer + backdrop OUTSIDE the <header> element (e.g., move them to a sibling/portal at document.body level), OR remove the `backdrop-blur-md/backdrop-blur-xl` from the <header> while drawer is open, OR replace backdrop-filter on the header with a `filter:none` workaround. The cleanest fix is to portal the drawer+backdrop into document.body via createPortal so the backdrop-filter on header no longer creates a containing block for them.
critical_code_review_comments: Navbar.jsx line ~47: header uses `backdrop-blur-md`/`backdrop-blur-xl` which establishes a containing block for ALL fixed-positioned descendants — this is what kills the mobile backdrop overlay. Same trap would affect any future fixed modal placed inside the header. Move modal/drawer overlays out of the header subtree.
Navbar.jsx: backdrop div has no data-testid. Add data-testid='mobile-menu-backdrop' for reliable testing of the overlay close-on-click behavior once the height bug is fixed.

server.py: live SMTP send is synchronous (smtplib) inside an async route — blocks the event loop for up to 15s on slow handshake. Wrap _send_email in asyncio.to_thread or use aiosmtplib to avoid blocking concurrent requests.

server.py: no rate limiting on /api/contact + now sending real emails on every POST = trivially abusable for outbound email spam from your authenticated Gmail account (App Password). Add per-IP throttling (e.g., 3 req/min via slowapi) BEFORE this goes public, or you risk Gmail suspending the account.

server.py logger ordering issue from iteration 4 still unresolved — _send_email references `logger` defined at module top, which is fine, but submit_contact catches Exception broadly and only logs — consider returning a 502-style hint via email_error in the response so the UI can show a fallback CTA when SMTP fails.

success_rate.frontend: ~93% (1 HIGH ui bug: invisible/unclickable mobile backdrop; all other 15 acceptance criteria pass)

test_credentials: N/A — no auth on this site. SMTP creds (hello@trackthebreach.com + App Password) live in /app/backend/.env.
seed_data_creation: Test docs inserted into contact_submissions collection with name prefix 'TEST_' + one 'SMTP Verify Test' doc — all auto-cleaned by module-scoped fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5: SMTP now LIVE — POST /api/contact really sends email via Gmail SMTP. test_contact_smtp_verify_live_delivery is the canonical test that emails will be delivered (sends 1 real email to hello@trackthebreach.com). All pricing/footer/navbar text and accordion behavior verified at 390x844. The one open bug is the mobile drawer backdrop overlay: it has height=0 due to `backdrop-blur-md` on the parent <header> creating a containing block. Fix path: portal drawer+backdrop into document.body via React's createPortal, OR move drawer JSX out of <header>. After fix, retest by clicking backdrop element to verify drawer closes and document.body.style.overflow returns to '' (currently only works via JS-dispatched .click()). All iteration-3/4 regressions (3+2 layout, 5 prices, INR toggle, sitemap.xml, blog detail, container 1240px, transparent logo, /api/contact persistence) still PASS.
rca_of_the_issue: Bug: mobile drawer backdrop click never closes drawer in real browsers. RCA: The backdrop div is a child of the <header> which has Tailwind class `backdrop-blur-md` (and `backdrop-blur-xl` when scrolled). The CSS `backdrop-filter` property creates a new containing block for descendants with `position: fixed` (per W3C Filter Effects Module Level 2 spec). As a result, the backdrop's `fixed inset-0 top-16` is resolved relative to the header's bounding box (not the viewport), and since the header has only its own content height (~88px) and the backdrop's top:64px > available height, the backdrop collapses to height:0. Reproduction: open https://risk-detect-18.preview.emergentagent.com on a 390x844 viewport, tap the hamburger, observe (a) no visible dim/blur appears over the rest of the page (b) tapping anywhere outside the rounded card does nothing. Verified via JS: getBoundingClientRect() returns {x:0, y:64, width:390, height:0} and getComputedStyle reports height:0px despite top:64px bottom:0px. Mitigation: render the drawer+backdrop via React.createPortal(<>…</>, document.body) so they escape the header's containing block. Quick partial workaround: increase header height to >> viewport (ugly) — DO NOT use. Proper fix is createPortal.
